name: Dell Latitude 5430 Rugged
weaknesses: Semi-rugged only — IP53, not IP66; limited water/dust protection
No hot-swappable battery — downtime during battery replacement
Lower screen brightness (1000 nit max vs 1400 nit Getac B360)
No dual battery option for extended field missions
Higher failure rate in extreme environments (8% vs 2%)
Limited warranty excludes accidental damage (drops, spills)

name: Dell Latitude 7330 Rugged Extreme
weaknesses: IP65 (not IP66) — less water jet protection than Getac
Heavier than comparable Getac B360 (2.72 kg vs 2.32 kg)
Warranty does not include bumper-to-bumper accidental damage
Limited display brightness compared to Getac 1400 nit options
No Wi-Fi 7 or AI Boost NPU on current generation
Smaller ecosystem of vehicle docking accessories

name: Panasonic Toughbook 40
weaknesses: Significantly higher price point ($3,899 vs $3,299 Getac B360)
Much heavier form factor (3.5 kg vs 2.32 kg)
Warranty does not include accidental damage coverage
Older modular design — slower upgrade path vs Getac
Limited display brightness compared to Getac 1400 nit
No AI-ready NPU or Wi-Fi 7 on current generation

name: Panasonic Toughbook 33
weaknesses: Highest price in category ($4,199)
IP65 only — lower water protection than IP66 Getac alternatives
Very heavy with keyboard dock (3.52 kg)
Limited warranty — no bumper-to-bumper accidental damage
Aging platform with no AI Boost or Wi-Fi 7
Smaller 12 inch display limits productivity

name: HP EliteBook 840 G10
weaknesses: Not ruggedized — no drop or water ingress protection
Very high failure rate in field conditions (15% annually)
Standard 1-year warranty only — very expensive to extend
No sunlight-readable display (400 nits typical)
No hot-swappable or dual battery option
Frequent replacements needed in harsh environments
5-year TCO vastly exceeds rugged alternatives

name: Zebra ET80/ET85
weaknesses: Only 1-year standard warranty (vs 3-year Getac bumper-to-bumper)
IP65 only — lower protection than Getac IP66
Limited computing power compared to full laptops
Older 11th Gen Intel processor
Higher TCO due to short warranty and expensive accessories
Smaller display (12 inch) limits field productivity

name: Samsung Galaxy Tab Active4 Pro
weaknesses: Android only — incompatible with Windows enterprise workflows
ARM processor — cannot run x86 field applications
Only 1-year warranty with no bumper-to-bumper option
Limited to 6GB RAM — insufficient for heavy enterprise apps
10% annual failure rate in demanding industrial use
No keyboard dock for productive data entry
No Thunderbolt/USB-C docking ecosystem
